description_long: This is a portable metal crowd control barrier commonly used in urban environments to manage pedestrian flow or restrict access. The structure consists of a rectangular frame with six evenly spaced vertical bars, all made from tubular metal, providing both strength and visibility. The base features two wide, curved feet that offer stability and prevent tipping, allowing the barrier to stand freely on flat surfaces. The metal surface has a matte gray finish, typical of galvanized steel, which resists corrosion and weathering. The ends of the barrier are blunt and squared, with no additional safety or energy-absorbing features. There are no visible reflective markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A gray metal crowd control barrier with a rectangular frame, six vertical bars, and wide curved feet for stability. The surface is matte and utilitarian, with no reflective markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile and the curved feet extending outward for stability.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full rectangular frame, six vertical bars, and both supporting feet.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the narrow profile and curved feet seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view identical to the front, showing the frame, six vertical bars, and both feet.
